mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% (near zero) signals an extended monetary easing regime that persistently compresses equity discount rates. This structural low-rate environment drives positive S&P 500 returns as investors rebalance into equities seeking yield, with the effect strongest over 20-day horizons where sentiment shifts dominate price discovery.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, equity valuations contract as forward earnings expectations decline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite UNRATE acceleration, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, this indicates demand destruction that cascades through corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 626833M and HOUST at 1702K as of 2007-06-15
regime_hint: demand contraction with synchronized weakness across consumer and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (RSAFS decline AND HOUST decline) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.89 reflects near-peak capacity utilization in a late-cycle expansion. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months, this signals supply-side constraints and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice profit margins downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth < 0.5% MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite INDPRO deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 281.93 combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. When CPI growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, the market reprices equity risk premiums upward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 281.933 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2007-07-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 149,629K reflects labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth. When PAYEMS declines by more than 100K month-over-month, this signals labor market deterioration that reduces forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and recession risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with employment losses
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite employment decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 19,805.96B indicates economic expansion that supports equity valuations. When quarterly GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, this signals late-cycle slowdown that compresses forward earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reduce growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,702K combined with retail sales (RSAFS) at 626,833M jointly capture consumer demand momentum. When both series grow above their 12-month moving averages simultaneously, this signals sustained demand strength that supports equity valuations and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as momentum investors recognize the signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both above 12-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1702K and RSAFS at 626833M as of 2007-06-15
regime_hint: demand expansion with synchronized strength across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.
